_meta.author: Claude (scorecard-refine-local autopilot)
_meta.note: Raleigh NC batch 1 (5): Mayor Cowell + council members Branch, Jones, Harrison, Melton (now Lambert-Melton). Evidence-based local-tier finalization. Roster web-verified June 2026: all five currently in office; Cowell/Branch/Jones/Harrison on the Nov 3 2026 ballot; Lambert-Melton is a lame duck (not seeking council re-election; lost the Wake County commissioner at-large Dem primary Mar 3 2026, term ends Dec 2026). Harrison is Mayor Pro Tem since Dec 2025. Scores reset to null where no evidence; FALSE only on a documented vote/statement/endorsement; TRUE only for documented affirmative alignment.

profile.education: B.A. Asian history, MBA (Wharton), M.A. international studies (Lauder) — University of Pennsylvania
profile.background: Mayor of Raleigh since Dec 2 2024 (won Nov 2024 with 59.8% over Paul Fitts 18.4%). Former NC State Treasurer 2009–2017 (first woman in the office; pension assets grew ~$60B to ~$90B, NC kept AAA ratings from all three agencies), NC Senate 2005–2009, Raleigh City Council 1999–2005; led Dix Park Conservancy. Born Memphis TN, 1968.

janet-cowell.notes_set: [2026-06-10 RESOLUTE local refinement] Roster verified current: sworn in Dec 2 2024; running for re-election — Mar 3 2026 primary canceled, faces Ashleigh Heath Armstrong Nov 3 2026 (Ballotpedia). HARD evidence: 2024 Equality NC PAC endorsement plus her own quote — 'I stood up against anti-LGBTQ+ discrimination on City Council, in the State Senate, and as Treasurer. As Raleigh Mayor, I'll keep fighting for equality!' — and she issued/shared Raleigh's June 2025 Pride Month proclamation as mayor (biblical_marriage q4 FALSE). As Treasurer (Dec 2016) she and the State Health Plan board voted to suspend the plan's exclusion of surgical/hormonal gender-dysphoria treatments (WUNC; biblical_marriage q2 FALSE). public_justice q0 TRUE on affirmative backing: endorsed 2024 by the NC Police Benevolent Association and the Raleigh Police Protective Association, and her INDY questionnaire backs 'competitive wages for officers' and Chief Patterson's reforms; March 2026 State of the City touted RPD vacancies at the lowest level since 2020. Economic: FY2026 $1.78B budget held the tax rate flat at 35.50¢ and she says she hopes downtown growth keeps property taxes from spiking, but the FY2027 budget raised the rate — mixed record, economic cell left null. Immigration (Nov 2025 federal operation): said the city was not informed of and not coordinating the operation and that RPD 'is not involved in immigration enforcement' while 'following the law' — non-assistance posture within NC law (which bans sanctuary policies); not scored, noted. Issued the June 5 2026 National Gun Violence Awareness Day ('Wear Orange') proclamation — noted, but no stance found on the specific self_defense policies, so null. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on abortion, school choice/parental rights, public prayer/displays, or election-administration cells — null, not penalized.

2.note: As State Treasurer (Dec 2016) she and the State Health Plan board voted to suspend the plan's exclusion of surgical/hormonal gender-dysphoria treatments for state employees (reversed by her successor).

4.note: Accepted the 2024 Equality NC PAC endorsement and pledged to 'keep fighting for equality'; issued/shared Raleigh's June 2025 Pride Month proclamation as mayor.

0.note: Endorsed 2024 by the NC Police Benevolent Association and Raleigh Police Protective Association; publicly backs competitive officer pay and Chief Patterson's reforms; touts cutting RPD vacancies to the lowest level since 2020 — affirmative advocacy beyond routine budget votes.

profile.religion: Baptist (member, Baptist Grove Church — serves in its Karygma Ministry)
profile.education: BS Electrical Engineering, NC A&T State University (2000)
profile.background: Raleigh native (Enloe High); service delivery manager at Kyndryl, formerly senior technical director at AT&T. District C council member since Dec 2015; Mayor Pro Tem 2017–2021 and 2023; chairs the Community Safety and Quality of Life Committee; sits on GoTriangle board and CAMPO. Briefly entered the 2024 mayor's race, then withdrew and defended his seat.

corey-branch.notes_set: [2026-06-10 RESOLUTE local refinement] Roster verified current: re-elected Nov 5 2024 (~40% over six challengers); advanced from the Mar 3 2026 District C primary with Jared Ollison — general Nov 3 2026 (Ballotpedia, Axios). HARD evidence: public_justice q0 TRUE — Oct 2024 N&O questionnaire says he would INCREASE the police budget for 'police officer pay and ensuring officers have equipment needed to protect Raleigh' and praises improvements under Chief Patterson (forward advocacy, beyond a routine budget vote; he also backs more ACORNS social workers and a non-police crisis-response unit — noted). economic_stewardship FALSE: voted YES as part of the unanimous council on the June 2026 FY2027 $1.76B budget with its 1.7-cent property-tax increase (CBS17) — though he favors a 'tax-neutral bond' for housing (INDY Feb 2026), noted as mitigation. refuse_state_overreach q2 FALSE: supported the June 2020 unanimous council vote imposing a citywide mask mandate (CBS17/WRAL). Marriage cells null: the Pride proclamation was a council-body action with no Branch-specific statement or dissent found, and no LGBT-advocacy endorsement of Branch was found — per discipline, not scored. Immigration (Feb 2026): committed to RPD 'upholding individual rights' and a 'safe, inclusive community' while agencies 'operate within the law' — no sanctuary advocacy, no cooperation pledge; null, noted. Active Baptist lay ministry recorded in profile; christian_liberty cells null (personal practice, not a policy position). NO EVIDENCE FOUND on abortion, schools/parental rights, elections, bail/crime cells beyond the above, or gun policy — null.

2.note: Voted YES (unanimous council, June 2026) on the FY2027 $1.76B budget with a 1.7-cent property-tax increase; he does favor a 'tax-neutral' housing bond (INDY Feb 2026) — mitigation noted.

0.note: Oct 2024 questionnaires: would increase the police budget for officer pay and equipment 'needed to protect Raleigh'; praises improvements under Chief Patterson — affirmative pro-funding advocacy beyond a routine budget vote.

2.note: June 2020: supported the unanimous council vote imposing a citywide mask mandate — imposed a COVID mandate on residents rather than protecting them from one.

profile.background: District E council member since Dec 5 2022; re-elected Nov 2024 (~52% over John Cerqueira III). ~10 years in the restaurant industry until a 2020 pandemic layoff; certified substitute teacher (2021). Civic path was Raleigh's Citizens Advisory Councils — chaired the Northwest CAC (2019), then the citywide CAC body; served on the Parks Board. Married, two daughters; Puerto Rican family heritage.

christina-jones.notes_set: [2026-06-10 RESOLUTE local refinement] Roster verified current: sitting District E member (District E, not at-large), re-elected Nov 5 2024 by ~1,400 votes; running Nov 3 2026 vs former councilor Marc Scruggs, primary canceled (Axios, Ballotpedia). Bio corrected: prior stub implied PTA leadership — NO EVIDENCE FOUND for that; she rose through the Citizens Advisory Councils. HARD evidence: biblical_marriage q4 FALSE — accepted the 2024 Equality NC PAC endorsement ('Christina Jones for Raleigh City Council - District E'). economic_stewardship FALSE — part of the unanimous council votes for the FY2025 and FY2027 (June 2026, $1.77B, 1.7-cent property-tax increase) budgets (WRAL/CBS17). public_justice q0 TRUE — her own platform plank: 'Invest in the retention of law enforcement and Raleigh Fire Department personnel' (affirmative advocacy; she pairs it with funding the Raleigh CARES non-police crisis program — noted). NO EVIDENCE FOUND on abortion, trans policy, schools/parental rights, prayer/religious liberty, elections, immigration, or guns — null, not penalized.

4.note: Accepted the 2024 Equality NC PAC endorsement — a single-issue LGBT-advocacy endorsement.

2.note: Part of the unanimous council vote (June 2026) for the FY2027 $1.77B budget with a 1.7-cent property-tax increase; the FY2025 budget also raised the rate unanimously.

0.note: Her own platform plank: 'Invest in the retention of law enforcement and Raleigh Fire Department personnel' — affirmative advocacy beyond a routine budget vote (paired with funding the CARES non-police crisis program, noted).

set.office: City Council, District D (Mayor Pro Tem)

profile.education: Ph.D. natural resource management; M.S. environmental and development economics
profile.background: District D council member since Dec 5 2022; re-elected unopposed Nov 2024; appointed Mayor Pro Tem Dec 2025. Coastal economics specialist with NC Sea Grant (NOAA program at NC State); faculty in NC State's College of Natural Resources and Dept. of Agricultural and Resource Economics. Chairs the council's Growth and Natural Resources Committee.

jane-harrison.notes_set: [2026-06-10 RESOLUTE local refinement] Roster verified current: sitting District D member and Mayor Pro Tem (raleighnc.gov, Dec 2025); running Nov 3 2026 vs Javon Smith-Cook, primary canceled (Ballotpedia, Axios). HARD evidence: biblical_marriage q4 FALSE — accepted the 2024 Equality NC PAC endorsement for District D. economic_stewardship FALSE — part of the unanimous June 2026 FY2027 budget vote with its 1.7-cent property-tax increase, and is advancing a new four-year ~$203M bond ('We have several bonds ending this year which frees up $203 million for a new four-year bond'); her 'fiscal responsibility with taxpayer dollars' questionnaire line noted. self_defense q1 FALSE — her own Oct 2024 public-safety Q&A: supports 'gun control legislation at the state and federal level' plus buy-backs and free gun locks. public_justice q0 TRUE — same Q&A touts 'we increased salaries for public safety workers like firefighters, 9-1-1 operators and the police' and hotspot policing of the transit center and Glenwood South credited with a 22% crime drop (public backing; her signature framing also diverts homelessness/mental-health calls from police to social workers — noted, not defund). NO EVIDENCE FOUND on abortion, trans policy, schools/parental rights, prayer/religious liberty, elections, or immigration — null, not penalized.

4.note: Accepted the 2024 Equality NC PAC endorsement for Raleigh City Council District D — a single-issue LGBT-advocacy endorsement.

2.note: Part of the unanimous June 2026 FY2027 budget vote (1.7-cent property-tax increase) and advancing a new four-year ~$203M bond; acknowledges 'hard choices about services and tax rates' ahead.

0.note: Her own campaign Q&A: 'we increased salaries for public safety workers like firefighters, 9-1-1 operators and the police' and 'we focused police resources on hotspots,' crediting a 22% crime reduction — public backing of police investment; she also champions diverting homelessness/mental-health calls to social workers (noted; not defund).

1.note: Oct 2024, her own words: 'Gun violence is an epidemic in our country and must be addressed' — supports 'gun control legislation at the state and federal level,' gun buy-backs, and free gun locks.

profile.education: B.A. political science, NC State; J.D., NC Central University School of Law
profile.background: At-large council member since Dec 2019 (re-elected 2022, 2024); former Mayor Pro Tem. Family-law attorney, NC Board Certified Specialist, partner at Gailor Hunt. First openly LGBTQ person elected citywide in Raleigh; founding board member of Stonewall Sports; married to husband Stephan (now uses the name Lambert-Melton). Not seeking council re-election in 2026; lost the Wake County commissioner at-large Democratic primary Mar 3 2026 (~18%); council term ends Dec 2026.

jonathan-melton.notes_set: [2026-06-10 RESOLUTE local refinement] Roster verified current but LAME DUCK: still a sitting at-large member (term to Dec 2026), goes by Jonathan Lambert-Melton; did not file for council re-election and lost the Mar 3 2026 Wake County commissioner at-large Dem primary with ~18% (Axios, Raleigh Public Record). HARD evidence: biblical_marriage FALSE x3 — married to husband Stephan and endorsed by the LGBTQ+ Victory Fund (2019, 2026) and Equality NC (q0); advocated and voted for Raleigh's Oct 19 2021 LGBTQ-inclusive nondiscrimination ordinance covering gender identity, with his own quote championing it (q2); calls himself 'a voice for LGBTQ+ and marginalized issues' with sustained advocacy (q4). economic_stewardship FALSE — part of the unanimous tax-increase budget votes and said in 2026 'it would be difficult to cut taxes or reduce government spending'; backed the $80M 2020 affordable-housing bond. refuse_state_overreach q2 FALSE — on council for the unanimous June 2020 citywide mask-mandate vote. refuse_state_overreach q3 TRUE — on the Dillon Rule limiting NC local governments: 'I would like to see it changed' (documented advocacy for decisions at the local level; credit given where earned). public_justice null/mixed — 2019 ACLU survey YES to an oversight board with investigate/subpoena/discipline powers, later supported first-responder pay raises and headcount and created the ACORNS unit, but no affirmative back-the-blue advocacy found — q0 null per the high-bar rule, noted both ways. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on abortion, school choice/parental rights specifically, prayer/religious liberty, elections, immigration, or the specific gun-policy cells (supports voluntary buy-backs only) — null.

0.note: Married to husband Stephan; first openly LGBTQ person elected citywide in Raleigh; LGBTQ+ Victory Fund-endorsed candidate.

2.note: Advocated and voted for the Oct 19 2021 nondiscrimination ordinance covering gender identity: 'I'm proud to see the Raleigh City Council come together to take action…'

4.note: Self-described ongoing 'voice for LGBTQ+ and marginalized issues'; sustained LGBT-advocacy endorsements (Victory Fund 2019 and 2026, Equality NC).

2.note: Part of unanimous tax-increase budget votes; 2026 questionnaire: 'it would be difficult to cut taxes or reduce government spending'; backed the $80M 2020 housing bond.
On council for the unanimous June 2020 vote imposing a citywide mask mandate — imposed a COVID mandate rather than protecting residents from one.

3.note: On the Dillon Rule limiting NC local governments: 'I would like to see it changed' — documented advocacy for decision-making at the local level.
